questions.text: What is the stated purpose of revealing the Book (Quran) in the opening ayah?
questions.answer: To bring mankind out of darknesses into the light by permission of their Lord.

questions.text: Why does Allah send every Messenger in the language of his people?
questions.answer: So that he may make the message clear to them.

questions.text: What was the message Musa (AS) was commanded to give to his people regarding the 'Days of Allah'?
questions.answer: He was told to bring them out of darkness into light and remind them of the Days of Allah (His favors and trials upon them).

questions.text: What was the specific favor Musa (AS) reminded the Children of Israel about?
questions.answer: How Allah saved them from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted them with worst torment and killed their sons.

questions.text: What is the famous promise of Allah regarding gratitude mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you; but if you deny, My punishment is severe.

questions.text: What would happen if everyone on earth were to disbelieve in Allah?
questions.answer: It would not harm Allah at all, for indeed, He is Free of need and Praiseworthy.

questions.text: What was the common reaction of the nations to their Messengers throughout history?
questions.answer: They put their hands to their mouths in rejection and expressed deep doubt about what they were called to.

questions.text: How did the Messengers respond to their people's doubt about Allah being the Creator?
questions.answer: They asked: 'Is there doubt about Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth? He invites you that He may forgive you of your sins.'

questions.text: What did the disbelieving nations say to the Messengers about being 'only human'?
questions.answer: They said, 'You are only human beings like us, who wish to avert us from what our fathers used to worship.'

questions.text: How did the Messengers respond to the threat of being expelled from their lands?
questions.answer: They relied upon Allah, stating that they would be patient with the harm inflicted upon them.

questions.text: What was Allah's promise to the Messengers regarding the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: 'We will surely destroy the wrongdoers, and We will surely cause you to dwell in the land after them.'

questions.text: Describe the drink given to the tyrants in Hell.
questions.answer: They will be given 'Sadeed' (festering water/puss) which they will gulp but hardly be able to swallow.

questions.text: What is the parable used for the deeds of those who disbelieve?
questions.answer: Their deeds are like ashes which the wind blows forcefully on a stormy day; they have no power over what they earned.

questions.text: Can Allah replace mankind with a new creation if He wills?
questions.answer: Yes, if He wills, He can do away with you and bring in a new creation; that is not difficult for Allah.

questions.text: What will the weak followers say to the arrogant leaders in the Fire?
questions.answer: They will say, 'Indeed, we were your followers, so can you avail us anything against the punishment of Allah?'

questions.text: What will Satan say to his followers after the matter has been decided on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: He will say Allah gave them a true promise, while he gave a false one; he had no authority over them except that he invited them and they responded.

questions.text: Whom does Satan tell his followers to blame on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: He tells them: 'Do not blame me, but blame yourselves.'

questions.text: What is the greeting of the believers in Paradise?
questions.answer: Their greeting therein will be 'Salam' (Peace).

questions.text: To what does Allah compare a 'Good Word' (Kalimah Tayyibah)?
questions.answer: To a good tree whose root is firmly fixed and whose branches reach into the sky, yielding fruit at all times.

questions.text: To what does Allah compare an 'Evil Word' (Kalimah Khabithah)?
questions.answer: To an uprooted tree from the surface of the earth, having no stability.

questions.text: How does Allah keep the believers firm in this life and the next?
questions.answer: With the 'word that stands firm' (the Shahadah/Faith).

questions.text: What have those who exchanged the favor of Allah for disbelief done to their people?
questions.answer: They have settled their people in the Home of Ruin—Hell.

questions.text: What are the believers commanded to do before the Day of Judgment arrives?
questions.answer: Establish prayer and spend from what Allah has provided them, secretly and publicly.

questions.text: What has Allah subjected (Sakh-khara) for the benefit of humans mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The ships, the rivers, the sun, the moon, and the night and day.

questions.text: What is the consequence if humans try to count the favors of Allah?
questions.answer: They will never be able to number them; indeed, mankind is most unjust and ungrateful.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) primary prayer for the city of Makkah?
questions.answer: To make the city secure and to keep him and his sons away from worshipping idols.

questions.text: How does Ibrahim (AS) describe the influence of idols on mankind?
questions.answer: He says, 'My Lord, indeed they have led astray many among mankind.'

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) settle his family in an uncultivated valley near the Sacred House?
questions.answer: So that they might establish prayer (As-Salat).

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) request for the people of Makkah regarding his family?
questions.answer: He asked Allah to make hearts among the people incline toward them and to provide them with fruits so they might be grateful.

questions.text: In his prayer, how does Ibrahim (AS) describe Allah's knowledge of our emotions?
questions.answer: He says Allah knows what we conceal and what we declare.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) thank Allah for in his old age?
questions.answer: For granting him Isma'il and Ishaq, despite his old age.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) specific prayer regarding his progeny's worship?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants.'

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) request for his parents and the believers at the end of his prayer?
questions.answer: Forgiveness on the Day the account is established.

questions.text: Should the Prophet think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do?
questions.answer: No, Allah is only delaying them until a Day when eyes will stare in horror.

questions.text: What will be the physical state of the wrongdoers as they run on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: They will be racing ahead, necks outstretched, heads raised, their gaze not returning to them, and their hearts void.

questions.text: What will people say when they see the punishment and ask for a second chance?
questions.answer: They will say, 'Our Lord, delay us for a short term; we will respond to Your call and follow the messengers.'

questions.text: What will be the response to those who ask for a second chance?
questions.answer: They will be reminded: 'Had you not sworn before that for you there would be no decline?'

questions.text: What will happen to the Earth and the Heavens on the Last Day?
questions.answer: The Earth will be replaced by another earth, and the Heavens as well, and they will all come forth before Allah.

questions.text: How will the criminals be seen on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Bound together in shackles.

questions.text: What will be the clothing of the people of Hell?
questions.answer: Their garments will be of liquid pitch (tar), and their faces will be covered by the Fire.

questions.text: What is the final summary of the Quran's message in the last Ayah of this Surah?
questions.answer: It is a notification (Balagh) for mankind that they may be warned and know that He is one God, and that those of understanding may be reminded.

questions.text: What is the 'Straight Path' mentioned in the opening of the Surah?
questions.answer: The path of the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy (Allah).

questions.text: What is the 'Woe' for the disbelievers?
questions.answer: A severe punishment in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the characteristic of those who prefer the worldly life over the Hereafter?
questions.answer: They are in extreme error.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His ability to guide or leave astray?
questions.answer: He leaves astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

questions.text: What is the 'patience' of the messengers in the face of mockery?
questions.answer: Their steadfastness and reliance on Allah despite the harm and rejection they faced.

questions.text: What is the 'victory' (Al-Fath) sought by the messengers?
questions.answer: Allah's judgment and support against the arrogant tyrants.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Loss' (Al-Khusran al-Mubin)?
questions.answer: The state of those whose deeds are like ashes in the wind.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'truth' in creation?
questions.answer: The heavens and the earth were created with truth (purpose and justice).

questions.text: What is the 'invitation' of Satan?
questions.answer: A call to falsehood and disobedience that has no real authority.

questions.text: What is the 'true promise' of Allah?
questions.answer: His promise of reward for the believers and punishment for the disbelievers.

questions.text: What is the 'yield' of the good tree?
questions.answer: Its fruit, which it produces at all times by the permission of its Lord.

questions.text: What is the 'stability' of the believers?
questions.answer: Their firm heart and mind anchored in the word of faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Home of Ruin'?
questions.answer: Hellfire.

questions.text: What are the 'partners' people assign to Allah?
questions.answer: Idols, leaders, or worldly desires they follow instead of Allah's guidance.

questions.text: What is the 'security' of Makkah?
questions.answer: The special status of the city as a sanctuary of peace.

questions.text: What is the 'valley' where Ibrahim left his family?
questions.answer: The valley of Bakkah (Makkah).

questions.text: What is the 'gratitude' of Ibrahim?
questions.answer: His continuous praise and prayer to Allah for all His gifts.

questions.text: What is the 'Day the account is established'?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'delay' for the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: A temporary period in this life before their final judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'staring' of the eyes in horror?
questions.answer: The shock of seeing the reality of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'void' heart?
questions.answer: A heart empty of hope and full of terror on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'decline' (Zawal)?
questions.answer: The end of one's power and life on earth.

questions.text: What is the 'replacement' of the earth and heavens?
questions.answer: The transformation of the universe for the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What are the 'shackles' (Asfad)?
questions.answer: The chains with which the criminals will be bound.

questions.text: What is 'liquid pitch' (Qatiran)?
questions.answer: A burning, sticky substance that will be the clothing of the people of Hell.

questions.text: What is the 'notification' (Balagh)?
questions.answer: A clear communication from Allah to all of mankind.

questions.text: What is the 'One God' (Ilahun Wahid)?
questions.answer: The central message of Tawhid (Oneness of Allah).

questions.text: What is the 'remembrance' for those of understanding?
questions.answer: The lessons and warnings of the Quran.

questions.text: How many favors did Allah give to mankind according to this Surah?
questions.answer: An uncountable number of favors.

questions.text: What is the 'language' of the message?
questions.answer: The tongue of the people to whom a messenger is sent.

questions.text: What is the 'darkness' (Zulumat)?
questions.answer: Shirk, ignorance, and misguidance.

questions.text: What is the 'light' (Nur)?
questions.answer: Faith, knowledge, and guidance.

questions.text: What is the 'permission of their Lord'?
questions.answer: The divine decree that allows people to be guided.

questions.text: What is the 'Praiseworthy' (Al-Hamid)?
questions.answer: An attribute of Allah, meaning He is deserving of all praise.

questions.text: What is the 'desire' for worldly life?
questions.answer: Prioritizing temporary pleasures over eternal success.

questions.text: What is the 'wrongdoers' (Zalimun)?
questions.answer: Those who reject Allah and His messengers.

questions.text: What is the 'purity' of the word?
questions.answer: The truth and clarity of the message of Tawhid.

questions.text: What is the 'stability' of the good tree?
questions.answer: Its deep roots and firm branches.

questions.text: What is the 'notification' to the people?
questions.answer: The Quran's call to all of humanity.
